name: Conversation tilhører præcis ét CustomerId
description: A Conversation belongs to exactly one Customer. In Layer 0, CustomerId is not on the Conversations table directly — it lives on ConversationPhoneNumbers.CustomerId and is passed via workContext.CurrentCustomerId at creation time.

layer0_evidence.table: ConversationPhoneNumbers.CustomerId (NOT NULL-ish — can be NULL in schema but enforced by business logic)
layer0_evidence.enforcement_point: ConversationController.CreateConversation — only phone numbers matching the user's customer can be used

gap: CustomerId is NOT stored on Conversations table in Layer 0 — requires JOIN via ConversationPhoneNumbers to resolve customer. Target model must store CustomerId directly on Conversation row.
target_invariant: INSERT Conversations must include CustomerId from workContext.CurrentCustomerId — enforced at handler level.

name: Creator er ALTID participant (Owner)
description: The user who creates a conversation must always be linked to it. In Layer 0, the creator is CurrentProfileId passed explicitly to CreateAndSendConversationMessage — not stored as a participant record.
gap: Layer 0 passes profileId at message-send time but does NOT insert a ConversationParticipants record. The Owner role concept does not exist in Layer 0.
target_invariant: INSERT ConversationParticipants (ConversationId, UserId=CreatedByUserId, Role=Owner) atomically with Conversation INSERT.

name: Participants må KUN være fra samme CustomerId
description: No cross-customer participant leak. In Layer 0, this is enforced by verifying the caller's profile IDs intersect with the phone number's allowed profile IDs — which is already scoped to a customer.

layer0_evidence.code: if (!numberAndProfilesIDs.ProfileIds.Any(id => userProfileIds.Any(uId => id == uId))) return ForbidWithMessage(...)

gap: Layer 0 enforces via profile intersection (profile → customer scoped). Target model: participants must have CustomerId check at INSERT time — validate UserId belongs to Conversation.CustomerId.
target_invariant: Before inserting ConversationParticipant: verify Users.CustomerId == Conversation.CustomerId for each participant.

name: Conversation kan IKKE eksistere uden participants
description: A conversation must have at least one participant (the creator). In Layer 0, there is no explicit check — the Conversation row CAN exist without a ConversationMessage in a failure scenario (partial state risk documented in FLOW_CC_001).

layer0_evidence.risk: INSERT Conversation (step 9) commits BEFORE CreateAndSendConversationMessage (step 10) — orphaned Conversation possible

gap: Layer 0 does NOT guarantee atomicity. Target model: wrap INSERT Conversation + INSERT ConversationParticipants in single transaction — if any step fails, rollback ALL.
target_invariant: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync wraps all INSERTs — same pattern as user_onboarding INV_003.

name: Duplicate conversation guard (idempotency)
description: If a conversation already exists between the same sender number and partner number, reuse it (send reply) rather than create a new one.

layer0_evidence.code: var existing = _conversationService.GetConversationReadModelBySourceAndPartner(...); if (existing is not null) { await SendConversationReply(...); return Ok(); }

target_invariant: CreateConversationHandler: check for duplicate before INSERT. Return existing ConversationId with specific error code (CONVERSATION_EXISTS) — caller decides to reuse or handle.

name: Authorization — only profiles with phone number access can create
description: CreateConversation requires the caller's profile to be in the phone number's allowed profile list. Cross-customer creation is blocked.

layer0_evidence.code: var numberAndProfilesIDs = _conversationService.GetConversationPhoneNumberWithProfileIdsById(command.ConversationPhoneNumberId); ... if (!numberAndProfilesIDs.ProfileIds.Any(id => userProfileIds.Any(uId => id == uId))) return ForbidWithMessage(...)

target_invariant: Handler validates: caller's UserId must have a profile scoped to Conversation.CustomerId. Role-based: require ManageConversations or equivalent role.
